tools.name: accounts_env_set-var-value
tools.description: Updates or creates a new value for an existing environment variable. Required: account_id, key. Optional: site_id, context, context_parameter (plus 1 more). Returns the updated EnvSetVarValueResponse.

params.name: key
params.description: The existing environment variable key name (case-sensitive)

params.name: site_id
params.description: If provided, update an environment variable set on this site

params.name: context
params.description: The deploy context in which this value will be used. `dev` refers to local development when running `netlify dev`. `branch` must be provided with a value in `context_parameter`.

params.name: context_parameter
params.description: An additional parameter for custom branches. Currently, this is used for providing a branch name when `context=branch`.

tools.name: accounts_env_update-var
tools.description: Updates an existing environment variable and all of its values. Existing values will be replaced by values provided. Required: account_id, key. Optional: site_id, is_secret, key-2 (plus 2 more). Returns the updated EnvUpdateVarResponse.

params.name: key
params.description: The existing environment variable key name (case-sensitive)

params.name: site_id
params.description: If provided, update an environment variable set on this site

params.name: is_secret
params.description: Secret values are only readable by code running on Netlify's systems. With secrets, only the local development context values are readable from the UI, API, and CLI. By default, environment variable values are not secret.

params.name: key-2
params.description: The existing or new name of the key, if you wish to rename it (case-sensitive)

params.name: scopes
params.description: The scopes that this environment variable is set to (Pro plans and above)

tools.name: sites_create
tools.description: **Note:** Environment variable keys and values have moved from `build_settings.env` and `repo.env` to a new endpoint. Please use [createEnvVars](#tag/environmentVariables/operation/createEnvVars) to create environment variables for a site. Optional: configure_dns, account_id, account_name (plus 36 more). Returns array of string.

params.name: build-settings-cmd
params.description: The build command to run. This is the command that Netlify runs to build your site. If a site has a netlify.toml file with a build command it will override this value.

params.name: build-settings-functions-dir
params.description: The directory where Netlify can find your compiled functions to deploy them. Defaults to netlify/functions if not set. You can also define and override this setting in your project’s netlify.toml file.

params.name: build-settings-stop-builds
params.description: When true, Netlify will not build your project automatically. You can build locally via the CLI and then publish new deploys manually via the CLI or the API.

params.name: published-deploy-functions-region
params.description: The functions region for this deploy as an airport code.

params.name: published-deploy-functions-region-overrides
params.description: Functions in the deploy that explicitly specify their own region (airport code).

params.name: repo-cmd
params.description: The build command to run. This is the command that Netlify runs to build your site. If a site has a netlify.toml file with a build command it will override this value.

params.name: repo-functions-dir
params.description: The directory where Netlify can find your compiled functions to deploy them. Defaults to netlify/functions if not set. You can also define and override this setting in your project’s netlify.toml file.

params.name: repo-stop-builds
params.description: When true, Netlify will not build your project automatically. You can build locally via the CLI and then publish new deploys manually via the CLI or the API.

Optional: account_id, account_name, account_slug (plus 35 more). Returns array of string.

params.name: build-settings-cmd
params.description: The build command to run. This is the command that Netlify runs to build your site. If a site has a netlify.toml file with a build command it will override this value.

params.name: build-settings-functions-dir
params.description: The directory where Netlify can find your compiled functions to deploy them. Defaults to netlify/functions if not set. You can also define and override this setting in your project’s netlify.toml file.

params.name: build-settings-stop-builds
params.description: When true, Netlify will not build your project automatically. You can build locally via the CLI and then publish new deploys manually via the CLI or the API.

params.name: published-deploy-functions-region
params.description: The functions region for this deploy as an airport code.

params.name: published-deploy-functions-region-overrides
params.description: Functions in the deploy that explicitly specify their own region (airport code).

params.name: repo-cmd
params.description: The build command to run. This is the command that Netlify runs to build your site. If a site has a netlify.toml file with a build command it will override this value.

params.name: repo-functions-dir
params.description: The directory where Netlify can find your compiled functions to deploy them. Defaults to netlify/functions if not set. You can also define and override this setting in your project’s netlify.toml file.

params.description: When true, Netlify will not build your project automatically. You can build locally via the CLI and then publish new deploys manually via the CLI or the API.

tools.name: sites_builds_create-site
tools.description: Runs a build for a site. The build will be scheduled to run at the first opportunity, but it might not start immediately if insufficient account build capacity is available. Files for build can be uploaded as a zipped site using one of these methods: 1. Set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip file as the raw request body 2. Set Content-Type to 'multipart/form-data' and include the zip file in the 'zip' field. Required: site_id. Optional: branch, clear_cache, image (plus 3 more). Returns the new BuildsCreateSiteResponse.

params.name: branch
params.description: If no branch is specified, it is treated as a production deploy If a branch IS specified and matches the main branch, it is also production If a branch is specified and doesn't match the main branch, it is a branch deploy

params.name: clear_cache
params.description: Whether to clear the build cache before building

params.name: image
params.description: The build image tag to use for the build

params.name: template_id
params.description: The build template to use for the build

params.name: zip
params.description: A zip file containing the site files to build. Only used with Content-Type 'multipart/form-data'. Alternatively, set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip as the raw request body (no 'zip' parameter needed).

tools.name: sites_builds_list-site
tools.description: List site. Required: site_id. Optional: page, per_page. Returns array of BuildsListSiteItem.

tools.name: sites_database_clear-site-compute-settings
tools.description: Resets project-level compute settings to tier defaults. Requires a Pro or higher plan. Required: site_id. Destructive.

tools.name: sites_database_create-site
tools.description: Creates a new database for the specified site. If a database already exists, returns the existing connection string. The database region defaults to the site's functions region if not specified. Required: site_id. Optional: region.

params.name: region
params.description: The region where the database should be created. Defaults to the site's functions region if not specified.

tools.name: sites_database_create-site-branch
tools.description: Creates a new database branch. If a branch already exists for the specified branch ID, returns the existing connection string. Required: site_id, branch_id. Optional: metadata, parent_branch_id.

params.name: metadata
params.description: Arbitrary metadata to associate with the branch

params.name: parent_branch_id
params.description: The ID of the parent branch to create the new branch from. Defaults to the production branch if not specified.

tools.name: sites_database_create-site-snapshot
tools.description: Creates a point-in-time snapshot of a database branch. Defaults to the production branch if no branch name is specified. Required: site_id. Optional: branch_id, metadata, name. Returns the new DatabaseCreateSiteSnapshotResponse.

params.name: branch_id
params.description: The ID of the branch to snapshot. Defaults to 'production' if not specified.

params.name: metadata-deploy
params.description: Deploy information associated with the snapshot

params.name: metadata-source
params.description: The source that created the snapshot

tools.name: sites_database_delete-site
tools.description: Deletes the database and all associated branches and snapshots for the specified site. Required: site_id. Destructive.

tools.name: sites_database_delete-site-branch
tools.description: Deletes a database branch. Required: site_id, branch_id. Destructive.

tools.name: sites_database_delete-site-snapshot
tools.description: Deletes a database snapshot. Required: site_id, snapshot_id. Destructive.

tools.name: sites_database_get-site
tools.description: Returns the database connection string for the specified site. Required: site_id. Optional: role.

params.name: role
params.description: The database role to use for the connection string. Defaults to netlifydb_owner if not specified.

tools.name: sites_database_get-site-branch
tools.description: Returns the database branch connection string for a specific branch. Required: site_id, branch_id. Optional: role.

params.name: role
params.description: The database role to use for the connection string. Defaults to netlifydb_owner if not specified.

tools.name: sites_database_get-site-compute-settings
tools.description: Returns the project-level compute settings for the database. Returns effective settings (custom or tier defaults). Requires a Pro or higher plan. Required: site_id.

tools.name: sites_database_get-site-migration
tools.description: Returns the contents of a named migration for the specified branch. Required: site_id, name. Optional: branch.

params.name: branch
params.description: The branch ID to look up the migration on. Defaults to the currently published deploy's branch.

tools.name: sites_database_list-site-branches
tools.description: Returns all branches for the site's database with compute status and metadata. Required: site_id.

tools.name: sites_database_list-site-migrations
tools.description: Returns the list of migrations available for the specified branch, indicating which ones have been applied to the database. Required: site_id. Optional: branch.

params.name: branch
params.description: The branch ID to list migrations for. Defaults to 'production' if not specified.

tools.name: sites_database_list-site-snapshots
tools.description: Returns all snapshots for the site's database. Required: site_id.

tools.name: sites_database_reset-site-branch
tools.description: Resets a non-production database branch by re-forking it from a source branch (defaults to the production branch). If the target branch is already in sync with the source, returns the existing connection string without performing a reset, unless `force=true` is passed. The production branch cannot be reset. Required: site_id, branch_id. Optional: force, role, source_branch_id.

params.name: force
params.description: If true, resets the branch even when it is already in sync with the source.

params.name: role
params.description: The database role to use for the returned connection string. Defaults to netlifydb_owner if not specified.

params.name: source_branch_id
params.description: The ID of the branch to re-fork the target branch from. Defaults to 'production' if not specified.

tools.name: sites_database_restore-site-snapshot
tools.description: Restores a snapshot to a database branch. Defaults to the production branch if no branch_name is specified. Required: site_id, snapshot_id. Optional: branch_id.

params.name: branch_id
params.description: The ID of the branch to restore the snapshot to. Defaults to 'production' if not specified.

tools.name: sites_database_run-site-migrations
tools.description: Runs database migrations for the specified deploy. Finds the deploy and determines the appropriate branch. Required: site_id, deploy_id. Optional: dry_run.

params.name: deploy_id
params.description: The deploy ID to run migrations for

params.name: dry_run
params.description: If true, validates migrations without applying them.

tools.name: sites_database_set-site-branch-compute-settings
tools.description: Sets compute settings for a specific database branch, overriding project-level settings. Requires a Pro or higher plan. Required: site_id, branch_id. Optional: max_cu, min_cu, sleep_timeout_seconds. Returns the updated DatabaseSetSiteBranchComputeSettingsResponse.

params.name: max_cu
params.description: Maximum compute units (0.25 to 16.0). Must be greater than or equal to min_cu. max_cu - min_cu must not exceed 8.0.

params.name: min_cu
params.description: Minimum compute units (0.25 to 16.0). Must be less than or equal to max_cu.

params.name: sleep_timeout_seconds
params.description: Seconds of inactivity before the compute endpoint is suspended. Use -1 for always on, or a non-negative value.

tools.name: sites_database_set-site-compute-settings
tools.description: Sets project-level compute settings for the database. Applied to new branches. Can be overridden per-branch. Requires a Pro or higher plan. Required: site_id. Optional: max_cu, min_cu, sleep_timeout_seconds. Returns the updated DatabaseSetSiteComputeSettingsResponse.

params.name: max_cu
params.description: Maximum compute units (0.25 to 16.0). Must be greater than or equal to min_cu. max_cu - min_cu must not exceed 8.0.

params.name: min_cu
params.description: Minimum compute units (0.25 to 16.0). Must be less than or equal to max_cu.

params.name: sleep_timeout_seconds
params.description: Seconds of inactivity before the compute endpoint is suspended. Use -1 for always on, or a non-negative value.

tools.name: sites_deployed-branches_list-site
tools.description: List site. Required: site_id. Returns array of DeployedBranchesListSiteItem.

tools.name: sites_deploys_create-site
tools.description: Create site. Required: site_id. Optional: title, deploy-previews, production (plus 14 more). Returns the new DeploysCreateSiteResponse.

params.name: environment
params.description: A list of deploy-specific environment variable data. Data specified this way applies only to this specific deploy and is merged into any existing environment variables set on the account and site. Deploy-specific environment variable data takes precedence over account and site environment variable data: For example, a deploy-specific variable with the key `NODE_ENV` will take priority over any existing site- and account-level environment variable data with the key `NODE_ENV`. Environment variable data may be provided at one of two times: - When creating a new Deploy with deploy files (most common) - When finalizing an existing Deploy with deploy files Once set, environment variables for a specific deploy cannot be modified. Subsequent attempts to modify environment variable data for a deploy will be ignored.

params.name: files
params.description: A hash mapping file paths to SHA1 digests of the file contents.

params.name: zip
params.description: A zip file containing the site files to deploy. Alternative to 'files'. To use this field, set Content-Type to 'application/json' and include the zip content here. Alternatively, you can set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip as the raw request body (not as JSON).

tools.name: sites_deploys_delete-site
tools.description: Delete site. Required: deploy_id, site_id. Destructive.

tools.name: sites_deploys_get-site
tools.description: Get site. Required: site_id, deploy_id. Returns the DeploysGetSiteResponse.

tools.name: sites_deploys_list-site
tools.description: List site. Required: site_id. Optional: page, per_page, deploy-previews (plus 4 more). Returns array of DeploysListSiteItem.

tools.name: sites_deploys_restore-site
tools.description: Restore site. Required: site_id, deploy_id. Returns the new DeploysRestoreSiteResponse.

tools.name: sites_deploys_update-site
tools.description: Update site. Required: site_id, deploy_id. Optional: commit_ref, async, branch (plus 9 more). Returns the updated DeploysUpdateSiteResponse.

params.name: environment
params.description: A list of deploy-specific environment variable data. Data specified this way applies only to this specific deploy and is merged into any existing environment variables set on the account and site. Deploy-specific environment variable data takes precedence over account and site environment variable data: For example, a deploy-specific variable with the key `NODE_ENV` will take priority over any existing site- and account-level environment variable data with the key `NODE_ENV`. Environment variable data may be provided at one of two times: - When creating a new Deploy with deploy files (most common) - When finalizing an existing Deploy with deploy files Once set, environment variables for a specific deploy cannot be modified. Subsequent attempts to modify environment variable data for a deploy will be ignored.

params.name: files
params.description: A hash mapping file paths to SHA1 digests of the file contents.

params.name: zip
params.description: A zip file containing the site files to deploy. Alternative to 'files'. To use this field, set Content-Type to 'application/json' and include the zip content here. Alternatively, you can set Content-Type to 'application/zip' and send the zip as the raw request body (not as JSON).

tools.name: sites_ssl_provision-site-tlscertificate
tools.description: Provisions or updates a TLS certificate for the site. **Creating a certificate (site has no certificate):** - Omit certificate params to initiate Let's Encrypt provisioning - Provide certificate, key, and ca_certificates to upload a custom certificate **Updating a certificate (site already has a certificate):** - REQUIRES certificate, key, and ca_certificates to replace with a new custom certificate - Use POST /api/v1/sites/{site_id}/ssl/renew to renew an existing Let's Encrypt certificate. Required: site_id. Optional: certificate, key, ca_certificates. Returns array of string.

params.name: certificate
params.description: PEM-encoded certificate. Required when updating an existing certificate.

params.name: key
params.description: PEM-encoded private key. Required when updating an existing certificate.

params.name: ca_certificates
params.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate chain. Required when updating an existing certificate.

tools.name: sites_ssl_show-site-tlscertificate
tools.description: Show site tlscertificate. Required: site_id. Returns array of string.
